title: Structured project knowledge in jit: addressable items, qualified ids, and projected invariants
description:
Make structured knowledge inside jit **first-class, addressable, searchable, and projectable**. Generalize today's regex-parsed requirements into a uniform model of *addressable items* with globally-unique *qualified ids*, add a project-scoped *registry* layer (invariants), and *project* invariants into agent-facing docs with drift detection. Requirements are one kind; the model is generic and domain-agnostic.

## Context

The validation engine is already generic over `(section, marker, id-pattern)` — `criterion_ids()` plus the `label-coverage` / `criteria-to-check` / `criteria-label-match` rules; "requirement" is just one configuration. Config already declares `[type_hierarchy]` and `[namespaces.*]` as registries, and `.jit/` already stores non-work-graph state (`config.toml`, `gates.json`, `rules.toml`, `schemas/`). This epic **names and consolidates** that latent pattern rather than inventing new surface area.

Two structural insights frame the work:
- Addressable items are an **annotation overlay on the work DAG**, not a second graph — coverage is computed by walking existing dependency edges. The work DAG stays the single first-class graph.
- jit holds **five data shapes**: nodes (issues), edges (deps), annotations (labels + addressable items), registries (config/gates/rules — and now invariants), and history (events/claims). Invariants are a **registry**, not work-graph nodes.

## Model

- **Addressable item** — a structured line in a declared section carrying a self-id (matched by an id-pattern), optionally a marker. **Qualified id `<scope>/<self-id>`**, derived not stored, where `scope` is an issue short-id or `@` for project scope (e.g. `eed6750c/REQ-01`, `@/INV-03`).
- **Item kind** — config-declared `(section, id-pattern, marker(s), link-namespace(s), scope, source-of-truth direction)`. No kind name hardcoded in engine logic.
- **Kinds shipped this epic:** `requirement`, `decision`, `risk` (issue-scoped, markdown-first, authored in issue/plan descriptions and indexed) and `invariant` (project-scoped, registry-first). This set spans both substrates to prove generality.
- **Link layer** — generic node→item label namespaces referencing qualified ids: `satisfies:` (requirement), `mitigates:`/`resolves:` (risk), `per:` (decision), `enforces:` (invariant). Existing `label-coverage` / `criteria-to-check` become instances over this model.
- **Invariant registry** — `.jit/invariants.toml`; each entry: `statement`, `kind` (`enforced` | `advisory`), optional `enforced-by` binding to a rule or gate key.
- **Doc projection** — invariants render into a **configurable** documentation target: either a delimited region of an existing file or a separate jit-owned file. The target path is config-driven; **no literal `CLAUDE.md` (or any doc name) is hardcoded** in engine logic.
- **Drift detection** — a validate rule reports **both directions**: declared-but-unenforced (an invariant whose `enforced-by` is missing/broken) and enforced-but-undeclared (a rule/gate no invariant claims).
- **CLI** — generic `jit item list/show/search/resolve` (filter by `--kind`) plus `jit invariant render/check`.

## Principles

- **Single source of truth, per-kind direction.** Issue kinds: markdown authoritative, index derived. Project kinds: registry authoritative, docs projected. Never a hand-maintained structured duplicate (avoid the type-declaration triple-source trap).
- **Domain-agnostic, opt-in.** Kinds are declared per project; nothing hardcoded in engine logic.
- **Graceful degradation.** A line with no self-id is plain prose; projects pay for addressing only where they want it.

## Success Criteria

- [hard] REQ-01: Every addressable item has a globally-unique qualified id `<scope>/<self-id>`, scope = issue short-id or `@` (project); the id is derived (not separately stored); self-id is human-authored and unique within scope.
- [hard] REQ-02: Item kinds are declared in config as `(section, id-pattern, marker(s), link-namespace(s), scope, source-of-truth)`; no kind name is hardcoded in engine logic.
- [hard] REQ-03: Source-of-truth direction is per kind — requirement/decision/risk are markdown-first (authored in descriptions, indexed); invariant is registry-first.
- [hard] REQ-04: `requirement`, `decision`, `risk`, and `invariant` are all implemented, indexed, and queryable, demonstrating generality across the issue-scoped and project-scoped substrates.
- [hard] REQ-05: Invariants live in `.jit/invariants.toml` with `statement`, `kind` (enforced|advisory), and optional `enforced-by` rule/gate binding.
- [hard] REQ-06: Invariants project into a configurable doc target (delimited region of an existing file, or a separate file); the target is config-driven and no documentation filename is hardcoded in engine logic.
- [hard] REQ-07: A bidirectional enforcement-drift check (declared-but-unenforced + enforced-but-undeclared) is runnable via validate.
- [hard] REQ-08: Generic node→item link namespaces reference qualified ids; the existing `label-coverage` / `criteria-to-check` rules are re-expressed as instances over the model or shown compatible.
- [hard] REQ-09: CLI exposes `jit item list/show/search/resolve` (with `--kind`) and `jit invariant render/check`.
- [hard] REQ-10: Indexes and qualified ids are pure projections; addressing degrades gracefully (a line without a self-id is not required to be addressable).

## Non-goals

- Per-item lifecycle/state records (L2).
- Cross-issue item→item dependency edges (enabled by global ids, but future).
- The `check`/`acceptance` and `glossary` kinds — declarable later via config, not built this epic.
- Modifying `jit-plan` (`eed6750c`) or other skills.

## Relationships

- **`56ab0224` (addressable structured items + qualified ids) is the foundational child** of this epic.
- **`jit-plan` (`eed6750c`) is a sibling beneficiary, no dependency edge** — it works at L0 today and adopts qualified ids / declared invariants opportunistically once they exist.
